steps.title: Apply the quadratic formula
steps.content:
For $2x^2 - 8x - 7 = 0$, identify $a = 2$, $b = -8$, $c = -7$.

Calculate the discriminant:
$$\Delta = b^2 - 4ac = (-8)^2 - 4(2)(-7) = 64 + 56 = 120$$
Apply the quadratic formula:
$$x = \frac{-b \pm \sqrt{b^2 - 4ac}}{2a} = \frac{8 \pm \sqrt{120}}{4}$$

steps.title: Match to the given form
steps.content: The quadratic formula gives two solutions: $\frac{8 + \sqrt{120}}{4}$ and $\frac{8 - \sqrt{120}}{4}$. One of these, $\frac{8 - \sqrt{120}}{4}$, has the form $\frac{8 - \sqrt{k}}{4}$, so $k = 120$.
